summary: Large camera-module decompilation pass. The PR replaced multiple camera.c placeholders with C implementations, improved many existing camera functions toward matching, and refined camera structs/prototypes so pause/debug camera and related state could be represented more accurately. The decomp.dev report credited the change with +4140 bytes and +0.11% matched code, including 8 newly fully matched camera functions, though it also reported a .data matching regression.
what_changed: Implemented or partially implemented a large set of functions in src/melee/cm/camera.c, replacing placeholders for functions including Camera_8002C1A8, Camera_8002C5B4, Camera_8002C908, Camera_8002E234, Camera_8002E490, Camera_8002EF14, Camera_8002F0E4, and fn_800301D0.
Added camera dependencies needed by the new code, including baselib/displayfunc.h, baselib/lobj.h, lb/lbrefract.h, lb/lbshadow.h, and mp/mplib.h.

Improved existing camera math in functions such as Camera_80029CF8, Camera_8002A0C0, Camera_8002B0E0, Camera_8002BAA8, Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D318, Camera_8002D85C, Camera_8002DDC4, Camera_8002F4D4, Camera_8002FEEC, Camera_8003006C, Camera_800304E0, Camera_80030E44, Camera_800311EC, Camera_80031328, and Camera_800313E0.

Introduced struct Camera_x2D0 in types.h to replace a run of individual Camera fields at offsets 0x2D0 through 0x300 with a named substructure for bounds, angle limits, pause camera distance limits, and a callback pointer.
Updated Camera_8002C5B4 to take Camera_x2D0* instead of void*, and updated Camera_8002C1A8, Camera_8002E158, Camera_800311EC, and Camera_80031328 prototypes in camera.h.
Added a forward declaration for Camera_x2D0 in forward.h.

Changed cm_803BCCA0 handling by exporting it in camera.h and commenting out its static initializer in camera.static.h; the diff excerpt shows this was part of a data/layout matching adjustment but the bot still reported a .data regression.
Added CameraDebugMode cm_80453004 as a non-static BSS symbol in camera.static.h.
Used helper macros and compiler-shaping constructs such as PAD_STACK, MAX, ABS, explicit float casts, scoped temporaries, and reordered local variables to improve codegen.
smart_moves: Recovering Camera_x2D0 was a high-leverage struct-layout fix: it grouped stage camera bounds, angle constraints, min/max pause camera Z distance, and a callback into one field of Camera, which made later camera functions express constraints directly instead of treating offsets as unrelated floats.

The PR used local scoping and temporary variables to influence register allocation and stack layout, for example in Camera_80029CF8 around Stage_GetCamInfoX24/Stage_GetCamInfoX20 and tangent calculations.
Replacing handwritten comparison blocks with MAX in Camera_80029CF8 simplified the source while preserving the intended max-distance behavior.

Camera_8002C5B4 was decomposed around vector operations: clamping position to bounds, applying x314 offset, normalizing cross products, and rotating pause_eye_offset around unit axes to enforce pitch/yaw limits.

Camera input handling in Camera_8002C1A8 separated digital buttons, analog stick thresholds, substick movement, zoom direction, and camera target switching, making a large pause/debug camera routine readable while still close enough for matching.
Header prototypes were tightened as functions became known, reducing use of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and void* where the diff had evidence for concrete types.
decomp_lessons: When a contiguous range of anonymous fields is repeatedly assigned as related stage bounds and camera angle limits, promote it into a named substruct; this can clarify call signatures and improve matching across many functions.
Do not assume old field names such as min_distance/max_distance are final: this PR replaced those with x2D0.unk28 and x2D0.unk2C once the surrounding struct was identified.

Small expression rewrites can matter for PowerPC matching: splitting multiplication chains, storing tanf results in temporaries, adding scoped locals, and using explicit casts to f32 were all used in matched/improved functions.
PAD_STACK remains a practical tactic for camera.c functions where source-equivalent logic still needs stack-shape padding to match.
Prototype cleanup should happen together with implementation work; changing Camera_8002C5B4 from void* to Camera_x2D0* and adjusting Camera_8002E158's argument list made downstream source clearer.
A PR can increase function matching while regressing data matching; track .data/.bss reports separately from code-function gains.
naming_conventions: Newly identified struct name used offset-based placeholder style: Camera_x2D0.
Unknown fields retained offset-style names such as x2C4, x2C5, x304, x305, x314, x32C, x341_b3_b4, x35C, x368, x378, and x37C.
Where field roles were clearer but not fully named, comments were added: x2C4 is marked as an unknown player slot and x2C5 as pauser slot.
Camera functions retained address-based decomp names such as Camera_8002E234 and fn_800301D0.
Struct field comments in Camera_x2D0 used offset annotations like /* +0x00 */ y_max and /* +0x30 */ callback.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(32), and PAD_STACK(56) in several camera routines to shape stack layout.
Used scoped local blocks to force temporary lifetimes and register allocation, e.g. info_x24/info_x20 in Camera_80029CF8 and idx in Camera_8002B0E0.
Reordered local declarations and swapped temporary variables to improve generated code in floating-point-heavy camera math.
Split chained expressions into intermediate assignments, such as scaling var_f31/var_f30 in Camera_8002A0C0 before multiplying by 10.0f and x2BC.
Used explicit f32 casts around M_PI_2 in HSD_ASSERTMSG comparisons.
Cached tanf(fov_u) in tan_fov_u and used a scoped tan_fov_l variable for horizontal frustum calculations.
Introduced unions for Camera fields such as x37C to allow both s32 and f32 access patterns where the diff showed mixed use.
Kept callback types explicit with void (*callback)(Camera_x2D0*) while casting known callback functions when assigning into x2D0.callback.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev reported 8 new full matches: Camera_8002E490, Camera_8002E234, Camera_8002EF14, Camera_8002F0E4, Camera_8002A4AC, Camera_8003006C, Camera_8002B0E0, and Camera_80028B9C.

decomp.dev reported 20 improvements in unmatched camera items, including Camera_8002D318 to 91.52%, Camera_8002C1A8 to 92.23%, Camera_8002C5B4 to 92.41%, fn_800301D0 to 96.94%, and Camera_8002CDDC to 95.68%.
decomp.dev also reported one regression: main/melee/cm/camera .data decreased by 240 bytes, from 75.21% to 37.23%.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching src/melee/cm/camera.static.h or cm_803BCCA0 to see whether the .data regression from this PR was resolved.
Search for later renames of Camera_x2D0 fields unk28 and unk2C, which appear to be pause camera distance limits.
Search for later PRs matching the remaining partial camera functions listed by decomp.dev, especially Camera_8002D318, Camera_8002C1A8, Camera_8002C5B4, fn_800301D0, and Camera_8002F4D4.
Look for review or follow-up discussion about why cm_803BCCA0 was exported and its static initializer commented out.
